what_it_does: Defines JSX components that render three compact UI kit thumbnails for Platform, Virtual Hub, and Vi workspace previews, with optional token-driven CSS custom property overrides applied at the wrapper level.
observations: The top-level component accepts an optional tokens map and converts entries into inline CSS custom properties.
Several token names are also aliased to semantic CSS variables such as --accent-gold, --bg-canvas, and --fg-primary.
The previews are composed entirely with inline styles and CSS custom properties rather than external classes.
The file defines a shared thumbnail frame plus specialized thumbnail components and small helper components.
The component is exposed by assigning KitPreviews to window.KitPreviews rather than using an ES module export.
standouts: Live token overrides are applied locally to the preview grid, allowing the same thumbnails to render a supplied palette.
The three thumbnails represent distinct product surfaces: dashboard, viewer overlay, and three-pane workspace.
The file uses miniature UI geometry, typography, borders, and fills as design-system probes rather than static screenshots.
conventions: Uses function components with JSX and inline style objects.
Uses CSS custom properties for color, typography, spacing, radius, borders, and shadows.
Uses array mapping for repeated miniature UI elements.
Keeps all helper components in the same file.
Relies on a browser global assignment for availability.
concerns: Inline styles make visual details dense and harder to scan or reuse inside this file.
The token override alias map is manual, so new token aliases would need explicit additions.
The file uses --accent-gold-faint in child components but the override map does not create that alias from the provided token names shown here.
The bg prop on ThumbFrame is optional but no local caller passes it.
notes: This file is a self-contained live preview renderer for checking how token changes affect several miniature ConceptV UI surfaces.
questions: Is --accent-gold-faint intentionally supplied only by the surrounding CSS environment?
Is window.KitPreviews the intended registration pattern for this project context?
Should the token alias map include every CSS variable used by the thumbnails?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it renders mini UI kit thumbnails inside a live preview area and makes them respond to token edits through CSS variable overrides.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make palette and token edits visible across several representative product surfaces without needing to load the full applications.
novelty: The file turns small JSX-built interface miniatures into a local design-token preview instrument, including manual token aliasing for compare-style rendering.
contribution: This file contributes a compact, token-aware preview layer that a fused system would keep as a way to observe palette effects across dashboard, viewer, and workspace interface substrates.
summary_for_embedding: A JSX component file that renders three inline-styled, CSS-variable-driven miniature UI kit previews with optional token override mapping.
intention_for_embedding: This was for visually testing live design-token palettes against representative ConceptV interface surfaces in a compact preview grid.

symbols.does: Builds optional CSS variable overrides from a tokens map and renders the three kit thumbnails in a grid.
symbols.description: KitPreviews is the top-level component for the file. It maps token names to inline custom properties, adds several semantic aliases, and applies them to a three-column wrapper containing PlatformThumb, HubThumb, and ViThumb.

symbols.does: Provides the common bordered thumbnail container with a label header and content body.
symbols.description: ThumbFrame standardizes the preview shell: rounded border, surface background, uppercase label, optional link text, and a padded content area. It accepts a bg prop for the frame background and renders children inside the preview body.

symbols.does: Renders a miniature Platform dashboard preview with sidebar items, toolbar buttons, and gallery tiles.
symbols.description: PlatformThumb composes a two-column dashboard mock: a small navigation list on the left and a Gallery content area on the right. It highlights one nav item with an accent border and uses PreviewBtn plus repeated square tiles to show a compact dashboard surface.

symbols.does: Renders a miniature Virtual Hub viewer preview with a gradient scene, logo bug, info panel, and menu control.
symbols.description: HubThumb builds a dark-overlay viewer thumbnail inside ThumbFrame. It includes a background gradient, a top-left ConceptV mark, a right-side apartment info panel, and a bottom MENU badge, all styled from inline values and CSS variables.

symbols.does: Renders a miniature Vi workspace preview split into chat, tasks, and output columns.
symbols.description: ViThumb uses a three-column grid to represent a workspace layout. It delegates repeated chat and task details to ViBubble and TaskMini, then adds a small output preview with gradient media, accent tiles, and muted text bars.

symbols.does: Renders a small inline button-like span with primary and secondary visual variants.
symbols.description: PreviewBtn is a miniature control used inside the Platform preview. The primary variant fills with the gold accent, while the non-primary variant is transparent with an accent border.

symbols.does: Renders a small chat-message row with an avatar mark and two muted text bars.
symbols.description: ViBubble is a helper for the Vi chat preview. The user variant uses a muted circular avatar, while the default variant uses a rotated square with an accent border, followed by simplified message lines.

symbols.does: Renders a small task row with active, done, and inactive visual states.
symbols.description: TaskMini is a helper for the Vi tasks preview. It changes background, border, opacity, and status-dot color based on active and done props while keeping the row represented as a compact task strip.
